name: Cannoli Siciliana
recipeIngredient: Marsala wine (5 tbsp) - or any other wine
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare cannoli tubes (metal cannoli forms; cut aluminum tubing into 5-inch lengths if commercial forms are unavailable).
2. Heat oil for deep frying.
3. Make the filling by combining:
- Ricotta cheese (2 cups, as fresh as possible)
- Superfine sugar (1/2 cup)
- Candied fruit (citron, orange peel, etc., finely diced) (1/4 cup)
- Pistachio nuts (coarsely chopped) (1/4 cup)
- Semi-sweet chocolate (optional, cut into fine dice)
- Vanilla extract or orange flower water (optional)
4. Sift flour and salt into a bowl, make a well in the center, and add Marsala wine and sugar.
5. Work the flour into the center until you have a firm dough, similar to noodle dough. If crumbly, add more wine; avoid making it sticky.
6. Place dough on a lightly floured pastry cloth or board and knead vigorously for about 15 minutes until smooth.
7. Form the dough into a ball, wrap in a clean, slightly dampened cloth, and let rest for 2 hours.
8. Roll out the rested dough on the pastry cloth or board to a thickness of no more than 1/16 inch.
9. Cut into 5-inch squares and roll each square around the metal tubes:
- Place a square diamond-fashion with one point toward you.
- Position the tube vertically across the middle, with ends at each point of the dough.
- Fold the sides over the tube, moistening the overlap with water and pressing together.
10. Remove the rolled shells from the oil when golden brown (use tongs as they are fragile) and drain on absorbent paper.
11. When cool, remove tubes and fill with ricotta filling.
12. Press the ricotta through a fine sieve into a bowl, then beat until creamy and smooth.
13. Beat in remaining ingredients with a large fork.
14. Fill pastry bag fitted with a large plain tube (or use nozzle without a tube) and pipe filling into cooled shells.
Makes 12 cannoli.
